log_entries.thought_preview: The malware is a PE32 executable, likely a spreader with overlay capabilities. I should investigate the sandboxes available to see if any of them have additional information.
The CAPA sandbox has some information, but it's limited. I should try to gather more information from the other sandboxes, specifically about network activity and dropped files.
The malware doesn't seem to have any network activity or registry persistence. However, it does drop files and create processes. I should investigate the files and processes further.
The malware drops two executable files, 0fhd84.exe and 3i41569.exe. I should investigate the processes created by these files.
The processes created by the dropped files are likely the main execution threads of the malware. The presence of rpcrt4.dll and shlwapi.dll suggests that the malware may be using Windows API calls. I

The malware doesn't seem to be using any registry keys for persistence. I should investigate the CAPA behaviors to see if they provide any additional insights.
